notes.anchor: M. CICERO PROCOS. S. D. APPIO PVLCHRO IMP.
notes.note: "Marcus Cicero, proconsul, sends greeting to Appius Pulcher, imperator." PROCOS. (pro consule) marks the office under which Cicero governed Cilicia; both men flag their titles, Appius his victory-acclamation as imperator.

notes.anchor: set out for a province with command
notes.note: Cum imperio: holding the formal military and civil authority of a provincial governor. Cicero took the Cilician command reluctantly in 51 BC as a consequence of Pompey's law requiring a five-year gap between magistracy and a province.

notes.anchor: by senatorial decree, the province must be administered
notes.note: Under the lex Pompeia of 52 BC and the Senate's enabling decrees, ex-consuls and ex-praetors who had not yet held a province were drafted to govern; this is how Cicero, consul a decade earlier, was now bound to Cilicia.
